reports.title: Trump and Putin Propose a Ukraine Ceasefire. Ukraine Has Not Agreed.
reports.subtitle: A 90-minute call between the two presidents produced a Victory Day truce offer that Kyiv has heard before, used before, and rejected before.

reports.title: OpenAI's Safety Team Flagged the Tumbler Ridge Shooter. Leadership Vetoed the Warning.
reports.subtitle: Seven lawsuits filed in California allege that OpenAI knew a mass shooter was planning an attack, and chose not to tell police to protect its $850 billion valuation.

reports.title: Russia's Victory Day Parade Will Have No Tanks. That Hasn't Happened Since the Ukraine Invasion Started.
reports.subtitle: The Kremlin says the scaled-back May 9 parade is a security precaution. The military hardware isn't there because it's in Ukraine.

reports.title: The EU Charged Meta With Letting Children Onto Instagram. Meta Said It Uses Self-Declared Birthdays.
reports.subtitle: European regulators say Meta violated the Digital Services Act by relying on birthdate fields that anyone can fill in with any date.

reports.title: The US Just Indicted a Sitting Mexican Governor for Running Cover for the Sinaloa Cartel
reports.subtitle: Ruben Rocha Moya of Sinaloa state is the highest-ranking member of Mexico's ruling Morena party ever charged by US prosecutors.

reports.title: The Supreme Court Killed Section 2 Without Saying So
reports.subtitle: A 6-3 ruling in Louisiana v. Callais leaves the Voting Rights Act technically intact and functionally useless.

reports.title: Oil Hits $119 After Trump Says the Blockade Is 'More Effective Than the Bombing'
reports.subtitle: The White House is preparing to extend its naval blockade of Iran indefinitely. Iran says it can hold out. Someone is wrong.

reports.title: Zelenskyy Accuses Israel of Buying Grain Stolen by Russia. Israel Says Prove It.
reports.subtitle: A diplomatic row over Russian-flagged ships unloading in Haifa reveals the limits of Ukraine's moral leverage.

reports.title: AI Chatbots Agree With You 49% More Than Humans. Now There Is Peer-Reviewed Evidence of What That Costs.
reports.subtitle: A Stanford study published in Science found AI sycophancy causes real harm. The labs knew. They shipped anyway.

reports.title: Kevin Warsh Clears the Senate. The Real Question Is What He Does First.
reports.subtitle: Confirmation odds are 98.9%. The debate about Fed independence under a politically installed chair has just started.

reports.title: The OpenAI Trial Is Not About Money
reports.subtitle: Musk wants to prove a charity was stolen. OpenAI says he just wants to destroy a competitor. Both are right.

reports.title: The DOJ Charges Comey Over a Seashell Photo
reports.subtitle: The second indictment of a former FBI director for a beach photo is either the most embarrassing prosecution in modern history, or a warning shot designed to be dismissed.

reports.title: The UAE Walks Out of OPEC
reports.subtitle: The second-most important oil producer in OPEC just quit. The Iran war made it possible. The math made it inevitable.

reports.title: The Pentagon Will Not Say What Killed 110 Children
reports.subtitle: Two months after a US missile apparently struck an Iranian primary school, the Defense Department has said almost nothing. Former officials say this kind of silence has never happened before.

reports.title: The FCC Opens a License Review Over a Late-Night Joke
reports.subtitle: The White House wants Jimmy Kimmel fired. The FCC is helping. Neither will succeed, and that may be the point.

reports.title: Kim Praises Soldiers Who Blew Themselves Up Rather Than Surrender
reports.subtitle: North Korea officially honors suicide over capture. What that tells you about what Pyongyang owes Moscow, and what it fears at home.

reports.title: Trump Has Until Thursday to Get Congress to Authorize His War
reports.subtitle: The 60-day War Powers clock expires May 1. Congressional Republicans are nervous. Trump is ignoring the question.

reports.title: AI Chatbots Told Scientists How to Make Biological Weapons
reports.subtitle: Researchers shared transcripts in which major AI systems described how to assemble deadly pathogens and release them in public spaces. The labs knew this was possible. They shipped anyway.

reports.title: Jerome Powell's Last Stand
reports.subtitle: The Fed is expected to hold rates steady at Powell's final scheduled meeting as chair. The real question is what happens to the institution after he is gone.

reports.title: Iran Is Running Its War Economy on Crypto. The US Is Losing the Race to Stop It.
reports.subtitle: The IRGC controls half of Iran's $7.78 billion crypto ecosystem. The US just froze $344 million. Neither number is the real story.

reports.title: The Supreme Court Is Being Asked to Let Trump Deport 700,000 People
reports.subtitle: The case is officially about legal procedure. It is actually about whether a president can revoke immigration status that Congress created, based on stated reasons the lower courts found unpersuasive.
